CalifOrnia
Fair POlitical
Practices Commission
Aprj.l 5, 1991
Darrell W. Larsen
sutter county CounseL
1160 civic center Boulevard
Yuba city, cA 95993
Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
our File No. I-91-0 8 5
Dear Mr. Larsen:
This letter is in response to your request for advice
regarding the duties and responsibi l ities of sutter County
Supervisor Peter Licari under the conft ict-of-i.nterest provisions
of the Potitical Reform Act (the trAct").1 Your letter does not
address a particular decision pending before your agency but
rather seeks general guidance. Accordingly, we are providing you
with inforrnal assistance pursuant to the provisions of ReguJ.ation
18329 (c) (copy enclosed) . z
OUESTTON
Do the confl ict-of-interest 
provisions of the Act disqualify
supervisor Licari frorn partj-cipating in decisions regarding ManueL
costa's application for a general plan amendrnent or the rezoning
of l,lr . Costa' s property?
CONCLUSION
Under the provisions of the Act, Ur. Licari nust disqualify
himself fron partj-cipating in decisions regarding Mr. Costa's
property if such decisions will have a reasonably foreseeable and
1 cor.rn."nt code Sections 81ooo-91015. At] statutory references
are to the covernnent Code unless otherwise indicated. 
commission
regulations appear at 2 california Code of Regulations Sectj-on
18ooo, et seq. AIl references to regrulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.
2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
irnrnunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Section 83114; Regulation 18329 (c) . )
428 J street, suite 8oo . P.o. Box 8o7 o sacramento cA 958o4-ogo7 . (gt6)3zz-566o


File No. I -91-08 5
Page No. 2
material effect on Mr. Licari,s property hrhich is distinguishable
from the effect of such decisions on the public generally.
FACTS
Mr. Manuel Costa owns a parcel of vacant land containing
approxirnatel,y seven acres. Two parcels have been separated irorn
this land; one for Mr. Paul ,s market and the other for a residence
situated near the intersection of Butte House Road and Elmer
Avenue. This land is located in the unincorporated community of
Tierra Buena, northwest of yuba City. 
The parcel has frontage on
both Butte llouse Road and Elner Avenue. !Ir. Costa has submitted a
request to amend the general pLan and rezone his l-and.3 
The
general- plan anendment request is for a connercial designation or
whatever other designation is appropriate or necessary to meet
general office requirenents. The rezoning request is to change
the designation of the property from AG generat agricultural
district 
to c-2 general commercial district. 
I{r. costa wishes to
erect an office complex on his 1and.
Supervisor Licari o!/ns a parcel of land which is situated 550
feet from the Costa property. llr. Licari,s residence is tocated
on this parcel. This property is designated Low density
residential in the generaf plan and is presently zoned R-1. The
county planning department has determined that the neighborhood in
which Mr. Licari's property is located is bounded by the railroad
on the $/est and south sides, the city linits of yuba City which
are just east of Blevin Road to the east, and young Road and its
extensions on the north. The planning departnent does not belj.eve
that there is necessarily a correlation between land uses on l1r.
Costa,s property and Supervisor Licari,s property. This is so
because the general plan has al-ready drawn a distinction between
the tlro properties, designating Mr. costa/s property for nedium
density residential- uses and Mr. Licari,s for 1o$/ density
residential use.
The planning department has also concluded that an eval,uation
of the financial impact on supervisor Licari,s property of any of
the conternplated changes on Mr. Costars property v/ould be
speculative. If Ur. Costa,s proposal j-s approved and the land is
developed for commercial offices, the traffic 
impact would be
prinariLy confined to Butte House Road and the southerly portion
of Elner Avenue. Thus, additional traffic might be introduced on
Butte House Road near Mr. costa,s property and similarly on Elmer
Road in the immediate vicinity of Mr. Costa,s property. 
The
planning department does not anticipate that traffic 
increases
generated by Mr. costa,s property would travel northerly towards
Mr. Licari,s property. Rather, it would be expected thit traffic
rrould travel east and south to and fron yuba city.
1' 
These facts conta in
you have subrnitted for inforrnation summarized from staff reports
our consi-deration.


File No. r-91-O8 5
Page No. 3
ANALYSI S
Section 87100 prohibits public officials 
from making,
participating in, or using their official position to inii,.uence a
governmental, decision in which they know or have reason to know
they have a financial interest. 
Members of the county board of
supervisors are public officials. 
(Section 82O4S.)
An official 
has a financial interest in a decision within the
meaning of Section 871oo if j-t is reasonably foreseeable that the
decision will have a naterial financial effect, distinguishable
frorn its effect on the pubtic generally, on the official, 
or on a
member of his immediate fanily,l or on:
Any real property in which the public official
has a direct or indirect interest worth one
thousand dollars (S1,000) or more.
section 87103 (b) .
You have advised us that Supervisor Licari owns real property
$rhich may be affected by the pending decisions. For purposes of
our analysis kre assume that his interest in this property is in
excess of $1,OO0. Consequently, Supervisor Licari must disqualify
himsel-f from participating in decisions regarding the Costa
property if it is reasonably foreseeable that such decisions will
have a material financiat effect on ltr. Licari,s property which is
distinguishable from the effect on the public generally.
Foreseeabi 1i-tv
The effect of a decision is reasonabl,y foreseeable if there
is a substantial likelihood that it will occur. To be
foreseeable, the effect of a decision must be more than a mere
possibility,' however certainty is not required. 
(Downev Cares v.
Downey Comnunity Development Comm. (L987) 1-9 6 Cal,.App.3d 983, g|g-
991; Witt v. Ilorro$/ (7977) 70 Cal.App.3d 817; In re Thorner (1975)
l FPPC Ops. 198 (copy enclosed). ) The Act seeks to prevent more
than actual conflicts of interest; it seeks to prevent even the
appearance of a possible confl,ict of interest. 
(Witt v. Morrow,
supra at 823. )
It is foreseeable that decisions regarding the Costa property
$ri11 have an effect on ttr. Licari,s property. Supervisor iiciri.,l
parcel is situated within 55O feet of the northern boundary of the
costa parcel. Mr. Licari,s five-acre parcel has frontage on Elrner
Avenue and so does the costa property. you indicate that the
character of the neighborhood where these parceLs are situated is
rural residential. 
The general plan designation for lIr. Costa,s
property is rnediun density residential use vhereas Mr. Licari,s
4 An official,s 
rrimmediate fanilyrr are his spouse and dependent
children. 
(Section 82029.)


File No. I -91-08 5
Page No. 4
parcel- is designated for low density residential use. It is
foreseeable that an increase in the use of the costa parcel, such
as the erection of office buirdings, !ri1r result in aiaitionar
traffic, 
an increase in pollution-and noise, and presumiUiy ""
increase in the denand for services such as fire i."t"-ii"i 
""a
police.which may lead to an increase or decrease in rand var-ues.
Thus, it is foreseeable that decisions regarding the costa
property will have an effect on litr. r,icari,s 1aid. If th; effect
is material, Mr. Licari rnust disguarify hirnserf rrorn faiiicip"ii"g
in such decisions.
Materia l itv
. 
For property located within 55O feet from the property which
is the.subject of the decision, the appropriate stairdaia f6,
deterruining nateriality is that.of negufation 18702.3(a) (3) (copy
enclosed) . The effect of a decision is naterial for prtpeity
Iocated outside a radius of 3Oo feet but within a rad-ius- of -,50o
feet of the boundaries of the property which is the subject of the
decisj-on if the decj.sion wilI have a ieasonably foreseeible
financial effect of:
(A) Ten thousand doLLars (g1o,OOO) or more on
the fair market value of the real property in which
the official 
has an interest; or
(B) WiIl- affect the rental value of the
property by $1,000 or nore per 12 rnonth period.
Regulation LB7 02 .3 (a) (3).
Mr. Licari must disqualify hinself fron participating in any
decision concerning the project that cour.d foleseeabiy in6rease or
decrease the fair market.val-ue of his property by $fO,OOO or more,
or the rental value of his property by at 1east g1,OOo in a 12-
rnonth period unless the t'pubtic Aeneri1lyr, exception applies as
discussed be1ow.
In rendering advice, the comrnission does not act as a finder
of fact and we cannot make a deterroination as to the material
effect of decisions regarding the Costa property on Mr. Licarirs
land- we must reave this factual deterrninalion of materiarity to
you within the guidelines provided by Regulation 1g702.3.
However, Regulation lA7O2.3 (d) does set iorth factors that you
rnust consider in deterrnining whether the decisions will hav6 a
material financial effect on the value of an official,s 
real
property.5 In order to make this determination, y""-r"=l-["X.
into consideration the following:
5 Th" factors to be considered are not rinited to the factors
specified in Regulation 18702.3 (d) .


File No. I -91-08 5
Page No. 5
(1) The proxinity of the property which is
the subject of the decision and the magnitude of
the proposed project or change in use in
relationship to the property in which the official
has an interest;
(2) Whether j.t is reasonably foreseeable that
the decision will affect the developnent potential
or i.ncome producing potential of the property;
(3) In addition to the foregoing, j-n the case
of residential property, whether it j.s reasonably
foreseeable that the decision will result in a
change to the character of the neighborhood
including, but not lirnited to, effects on traffic,
vier.r, privacy, intensity of use, noise 1evel,s, air
ernissions, or sirnilar traits of the neighborhood.
Regulation 18702.3 (d) .
Mr. Licari must make a good faith effort to determine the
financiaL effect of the proposed decisions on his land. you have
stated in your reguest for advice that land in the vicinity of
these two parcels sol-d for approximately $a.23 to $3.50 per square
foot in May of 1990. This is not sufficient information to aid in
the determination of whether decisions regarding the Costa
property $ri11 result in an increase or decrease in the value of
Mr. Licari's property of $10,ooo or tnore, or whether the rental
value of !lr. Licari,s land will i.ncrease or decrease by $1,OOO in
a twelve-month period. Mr. Licarj-rs property is currently used
for residential purposes. Considerations such as the change in
the character of the neighborhood in which his property is located
r^rith respect to traffic, 
view, privacy, intensity of use, noise
levels, air emissions, or similar traits of the neighborhood are
factors which rnust be considered in determining the financial
effect the decisions will have on Mr. Licari,s property. yet
another consideration r^roufd be whether it is foreseeable that
completion of the office complex wj-l-I increase the likelihood that
Mr. Licari will develop all or part of his f i','e acres thus
increasing the value of his land.
PubLic Generallv
Even when the effect cI a decision hriIl be material-, a public
official 
nay participate -.n decisions which eril-1 have an effect on
the public official 
wh'-ch is substantially similar to the effect
of the decision on tl^e public generally. 
(Regulation 18703, copy
enclosed. )
For the t'public generally'r exception to app1y, a decision
must af fec+- the public official,s 
interest in a rnanner which is
-. 
--ntially 
similar to the effect of the decision on a
s 
. ant segment of the residents and persons doing business in
the 
-sdiction. 
The public constitutes at1 the residents of the


File No. I-91-O8 5
Page No. 6
jurisdiction. 
(rn re.Lecran (1985) 9 FppC Ops. 1, copy encl,osed. )
In the case of Supervisor Licari, the public consists of al-l the
residents of Sutter County. Where the official,s 
property is
betr{een 30O and 2,500 feet of the property that is the suLject of
the decj,sions, he must show that all the properties that aie
roughLy the same distance away from the proposed project are
sinilarly affected and that these property owners constitute a
significant segrrent of the population, or that the effect on the
remainder of the population will be substantially the same as the
effect on him. (Cosqrove Advice Letter, No. I-89-178, copy
enclosed. )
We have insufficient infornation to determine hrhether the
trpublic generalLyrr exception would apply to the facts as presented
in your letter. 
However, it would appear that, because Ur.
Licari's property is located a short distance from the property
which is the subject of the pending decisions, the effect ot such
decisions on llr. Licari,s property wil-1 be significantly different
from the effect of the decisions on residents of the county whose
properties are located at a greater distance. For exanple, an
increase in traffic density in the area surrounding the proposed
office buildings will inpact Mr. Licari,s residence in a nanner
which i.s distinguishable fron the effect of this traffic 
on
residents further removed fron the neighborhood.
Additionally, Mr. Licari ovrns five acres of 1and. We have
previously advised that a decision wiLl- affect a public official,s
interests in the same manner as it wiII affect the public
generally if it can be shor^rn that persons who hold acreage within
the jurisdiction which is sirnilar to the acreage held by the
public official 
constitute a significant segrnent of the public.
(Sexton Advice Letter, No. A-91--023, copy enclosed.) We have no
facts that would indicate that a significant segrent of the
population of Sutter County owns five acres of Land.
Thus, it is IikeIy that the public generally exception does
not appJ.y. Therefore, if decisions regarding the Costa property
will have a material financiaL effect on Mr. Licari,s property,
llr. Licari rnust disqualify frorn participating in these decisions.
we trust this letter adequately responds to your inquiry.
Should you have any further questions regarding this natter, do
not hesitate to call ne at (916) 322-59oL,
Sincerely,
Scott HaIlabrin
Acting General Counsel

QUESTION: Do the confl ict-of-interest 
provisions of the Act disqualify
